caption: Some behavior gets easier to spot once it has a name. #socialintelligence #wordchoice #verbsy
title: people who drain your peace

words.definition: often complaining in a sharp, dissatisfied way.
words.example: Everything becomes a problem around them.

words.definition: irritable, difficult, and quick to argue.
words.example: The mood changes the second they enter.

caption: Quiet feelings still deserve precise language. #emotionalvocabulary #selfawareness #verbsy
title: feelings you hide too well

words.definition: the habit of keeping your thoughts or feelings private.
words.example: Silence, but with a locked door behind it.

words.definition: a deep unease or dissatisfaction you cannot settle.
words.example: When nothing fits, including your own mood.

words.definition: a heavy, thoughtful sadness that lingers.

caption: Anger has levels. These words make that obvious. #wordchoice #communication #verbsy
title: stop saying very angry

words.example: Too angry to sound casual anymore.
A short fuse disguised as a personality.

words.definition: furious to the point of losing control.
words.example: When anger takes over the whole face.

caption: Use these when your point needs to land cleanly. #articulate #wordchoice #verbsy
title: dangerously articulate words

words.example: A trenchant comment makes the room pause.

words.definition: clear, easy to understand, and mentally sharp.
words.example: The best ideas feel lucid, not loud.
A deft reply fixes the tension without force.

caption: Confidence is better when it does not need to announce itself. #mindsetwords #communication #verbsy
title: quiet confidence words

words.example: The kind of calm that unsettles chaos.

words.definition: composed confidence in a difficult moment.
mental calm, even when things get difficult.
words.example: Peace that does not collapse on contact.
